Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1) Use search_emails (query: "Brian Foster" or "brian.foster") to find Brian's recent communications. Agent discovers email_90701 (Brian to Grace Yamamoto, April 17, subject "Quick question about Keystone partnership") describing vague cost concerns on recent referrals, and email_90702 (Grace to Jordan Blake, April 20, forwarding Brian's email with "Do not loop Carlos in yet").
2) Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Brian Foster" or "Foster") to search Slack for Brian-related discussions. Agent finds old Slack messages from existing scenarios but no new injected messages with specific billing details.
3) Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Brian Foster") to look up Brian's contact info. Agent finds realtor_41de0baa1c6d: Brian Foster, brian.foster@mac.com, (980) 850-3029, Keller Williams.
4) Use crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Brian Foster") or crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: ["realtor_41de0baa1c6d"]) to find CRM records for Brian. Agent discovers crm_engagement_98d75f439205 (Jordan Blake's NOTE, April 21): "Brian reached out to Grace with concerns about closing costs on recent referrals. Seems like at least one deal in the Steele Creek area was the main issue. Need to review recent Carlos Rivera closings where Brian was the referring agent."
5) Use mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Brian Foster" or "Steele Creek") to find loans where Brian was the referring realtor. Agent discovers LN-2026-00650 (Tessa Dunlap, $310K FHA purchase at 3912 Steele Creek Rd, closed April 10, Carlos Rivera LO, Sofia Reyes processor).
6) Use mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id: "los_loan_5923d69dda87") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00650") to get full loan details. Agent sees closed FHA loan, Brian Foster as realtor, borrower Tessa Dunlap (tessa.dunlap@gmail.com).
7) Use stripe_search_customers (query: "Tessa Dunlap" or "Dunlap") or stripe_search_charges (query: "Dunlap" or "LN-2026-00650") to find Stripe payment records for the borrower. Agent finds customer cus_5923d69dda8798d7 (Tessa Dunlap).
8) Use stripe_list_charges (customer_id: "cus_5923d69dda8798d7") to list all charges for Tessa Dunlap. Agent discovers 3 charges: ch_5f43920505261a87 ($55 credit report, March 5), ch_a5dfa0a44176003b ($475 appraisal original, March 6), ch_54d8b711c22c281b ($475 appraisal DUPLICATE, March 6, 1 hour later). Two identical $475 charges equal the billing error. Neither has been refunded.
9) Use mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id: "los_loan_5923d69dda87") to check the loan's activity feed. Agent finds los_activity_05261a87a5df (Sofia Reyes, April 7): "Post-processing flag: Borrower Tessa Dunlap reported a duplicate appraisal charge on her credit card statement. Two charges of $475.00 both dated March 6. Left voicemail for Carlos on 4/6. No response as of 4/7."
10) Use stripe_create_refund (charge_id: "ch_54d8b711c22c281b", reason: "duplicate") to refund the duplicate $475.00 appraisal charge to Tessa Dunlap. This is the "make it right" action from the prompt.
11) Use send_email (sender: "carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["brian.foster@mac.com"], subject: related to the partnership/Tessa situation, content: honest message acknowledging the dropped ball, identifying the specific fee issue and its resolution, proposing next steps for the relationship) to email Brian from Carlos. Content should reference: Tessa Dunlap, the duplicate charge, the refund, Carlos's responsibility for the delay, and commitment to the partnership.
12) Use mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_5923d69dda87", action: "note", detail: documenting the duplicate charge resolution and refund, author: "Carlos Rivera") to log the resolution on the Dunlap loan file.
13) Use crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: documenting the Brian Foster partner situation, resolution steps, and current risk status, contact_ids: [Brian's CRM contact ID]) to update CRM records so the BD team has visibility.
14) Use conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: message about the Brian Foster referral partner situation, what happened, what was resolved, and the risk to the partnership) to flag the situation in the sales pipeline channel.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✗Run #1The email to Brian Foster discusses a rate lock extension fee ($350) on the Katz file and a high application deposit on the Hassan file, but contains no mention of a duplicate charge, double charge, or $475 appraisal fee — the actual billing error on the Dunlap loan.
✗Run #2The email to Brian Foster does not reference a $475 duplicate appraisal charge or any billing error on the Dunlap loan. The agent fabricated a different issue ($350 rate lock extension on a different borrower) and the email references that instead. No mention of 'duplicate,' '$475,' or 'appraisal fee' error appears.
